author: Pavlo Myroniuk

name: Photo-tagging

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* feat: open tagger in the same tab saving navigation history; by @TheBestTvarynka in https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/pull/22
* feat(ui): set icon to 'user-round-search'; by @TheBestTvarynka in https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/pull/23
* feat: restore tagger state after Obsidian app restart; by @TheBestTvarynka in https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/pull/24
* feat(ui): improve tagger ui/ux; by @TheBestTvarynka in https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/pull/25
* fix(ui): tagger: fix image container height; by @TheBestTvarynka in https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/pull/26
* chore: v.0.3.1; by @TheBestTvarynka in https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/pull/27


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/compare/0.3.0...0.3.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

This release contains a breaking change. After the plugin upgrade, the user must run this command inside Obsidian: `Photo-tagging: Generate deep-zoom tiles for all tagged photos`.

Now photo tagger automatically generates image tiles (image pyramids) and saves them inside the `./obsidian/plugin/photo-tagging/tiles/<vault/image/path>/`. These tiles are used during image zoom to handle large images.

The command will generate the required tiles for all images listed in `photo-tags.json`.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> You must have [`libvips`](https://github.com/libvips/libvips) installed. See installation instructions: https://www.libvips.org/install.html.

`libvips` is used to generate image tiles (image pyramids). This feature cannot be disabled.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/photo-tagging/compare/0.2.3...0.3.0

releases.author: TheBestTvarynka
releases.description:
# `hashtag`s

Now the user can specify image hashtags. The main difference from regular tags is that many hashtags can be assigned to many images. Then, the user can group a person's photos by hashtag.
releases.name: v.0.2.0
